question: How long do I have to dispute a charge on my checking account, and is there a fee to file a dispute?

sources.title: Card & Account Dispute Policy
sources.excerpt: Customers may dispute an unauthorized or billing-error charge within 60 days of the statement date on which the charge first appeared. There is no fee to file a dispute.

sources.title: Everyday Checking Terms
sources.excerpt: Standard checking accounts carry no charge for dispute submission; provisional credit may be issued within 10 business days.

sources.title: Online dispute portal status
sources.excerpt: The online dispute portal is operating normally as of 2026-07-17.

sources.title: Filing a dispute (archived)
sources.excerpt: Disputes must be filed within 30 days of the transaction.

sources.title: Everyday Checking Terms (draft update)
sources.excerpt: Draft: proposed change extending the provisional-credit window to 15 business days.
